# Intake Summary: Margaret Holloway
**Intake ID:** demo-intake-001
**Submitted:** April 21, 2025
**Classification:** Probate (95% confidence)
**Fit Status:** IN SCOPE
**Urgency:** HIGH

---

## What the Prospective Client Says

My father, Robert Holloway, passed away on March 3rd of this year. He had a will that was executed about eight years ago naming me as the executor. My sister, Patricia Greer, is now contesting the will. She claims that our father lacked mental capacity when he signed it, and that I unduly influenced him. The estate includes the family home here in Madison (appraised around $280,000) and a joint checking account with about $98,000. There is also a small investment account but we have not gotten a valuation yet. I have the original will and I have already notified the probate court. My sister has hired an attorney. I need to know what to do next and whether I need an attorney to defend the will.

---

## Classification Rationale

The matter description appears to involve Probate. The classification is based on keyword and context analysis of the intake form. Confidence score: 95/100.
Secondary areas potentially implicated: Estate Planning, Small Business Disputes.

---

## Missing Information

- Confirm whether prior counsel was involved on this specific matter.
- Copy of the will and any codicils needed for review.
- Inventory or estimate of all estate assets and liabilities.

---

## Conflict-Check Names

*This name list is extracted for conflict-screening purposes only. It may be incomplete or inaccurate. An attorney must conduct a full conflict-of-interest review using the firm's client database before any substantive discussion occurs. This output does not represent a conflict clearance.*

- **Margaret Holloway** (client)
- **Robert Holloway** (referenced_third_party)
- **Patricia Greer (sister** (referenced_third_party)
- **opposing party)** (opposing_party)
- **Greer's attorney name unknown** (referenced_third_party)

---

## Suggested Consultation Questions

- Can you walk me through the timeline of events from beginning to today?
- Have you been contacted by any other attorney about this matter?
- Have you signed any documents related to this matter that we should review?
- Has the will been filed with the probate court? Which county?
- What is your sister's specific basis for contesting the will — capacity, undue influence, or both?
- Are there any other potential beneficiaries or interested parties beyond those mentioned?
- Has any asset been transferred or distributed since the date of death?

---

## Internal Warnings

- ⚠️ HIGH URGENCY: Demand letter or time-sensitive trigger mentioned. Verify applicable deadlines.
- ⚠️ HIGH VALUE: Estimated value exceeds $100,000. Senior attorney review recommended before accepting engagement.

---

*This summary is a draft prepared by AI-assisted intake processing. It does not constitute legal advice and requires attorney review before any action is taken. No attorney-client relationship has been formed.*

intake.matter_description: I own a landscaping business, Mendez Landscaping LLC. In October 2024 I entered into a written contract with Ridgeline Properties LLC to provide seasonal landscaping services for a commercial property they manage at 400 Commerce Parkway. I completed all work as specified and submitted my final invoice on November 15, 2024 for $28,500. They acknowledged receipt of the invoice but have not paid. I have sent two follow-up emails and left voicemails with their property manager, Jordan Blake, and their accounting contact, Sandra Odom. It has now been over 90 days. I have all the paperwork — the signed contract, the signed completion report, and all invoice and communication records. I want to send a formal demand letter and pursue collection if necessary.
intake.parties_involved: Mendez Landscaping LLC (client business), Carlos Mendez (owner), Ridgeline Properties LLC (opposing party), Jordan Blake (property manager), Sandra Odom (accounting contact)

# Intake Summary: Carlos Mendez
**Intake ID:** demo-intake-002
**Submitted:** April 22, 2025
**Classification:** Small Business Disputes (95% confidence)
**Fit Status:** IN SCOPE
**Urgency:** HIGH

---

## What the Prospective Client Says

I own a landscaping business, Mendez Landscaping LLC. In October 2024 I entered into a written contract with Ridgeline Properties LLC to provide seasonal landscaping services for a commercial property they manage at 400 Commerce Parkway. I completed all work as specified and submitted my final invoice on November 15, 2024 for $28,500. They acknowledged receipt of the invoice but have not paid. I have sent two follow-up emails and left voicemails with their property manager, Jordan Blake, and their accounting contact, Sandra Odom. It has now been over 90 days. I have all the paperwork — the signed contract, the signed completion report, and all invoice and communication records. I want to send a formal demand letter and pursue collection if necessary.

---

## Classification Rationale

The matter description appears to involve Small Business Disputes. The classification is based on keyword and context analysis of the intake form. Confidence score: 95/100.
Secondary areas potentially implicated: Real Estate Transactions.

---

## Missing Information

- Confirm whether prior counsel was involved on this specific matter.
- Copy of the written contract referenced.
- Invoice number(s) and invoice date(s).
- Records of all communications regarding non-payment.

---

## Conflict-Check Names

*This name list is extracted for conflict-screening purposes only. It may be incomplete or inaccurate. An attorney must conduct a full conflict-of-interest review using the firm's client database before any substantive discussion occurs. This output does not represent a conflict clearance.*

- **Carlos Mendez** (client)
- **Mendez Landscaping LLC** (entity)
- **Ridgeline Properties LLC** (opposing_party)
- **Jordan Blake** (referenced_third_party)
- **Sandra Odom** (referenced_third_party)

---

- Has the other party given any written reason for non-payment?
- What is the nature of the business relationship — is this a one-time project or ongoing?
- Are you seeking payment only, or also damages for breach of contract?

---

- ⚠️ HIGH URGENCY: Demand letter or time-sensitive trigger mentioned. Verify applicable deadlines.
- ⚠️ DEMAND LETTER: Active demand mentioned — potential for imminent litigation.
